questions.question: A production possibility curve shows
options: How much of the resources of society are used to produce a particular commodity
The rate of unemployment in the economy
The various combination of two commodities that can be produced

questions.question: The study of the economic behaviour of individual decision-making units (consumers resources owners and business firms) in a free-enterprise economy is known as
Which of the following best describes the concept of opportunity cost?
options: A special bargain or sale at below market price
Costs for inputs tend to go up as we use more of them
Goods that are not produced in order to produce more of another good

questions.question: Market is defined as
options: One geographical location where people meet to buy and sell
The demand and supply of goods and services
A group of people whose sole interest is to make profit
Any organization or framework which links the buyers and sellers of a good or service.

Labour productivity is defined as
options: The maximum number of hour worked

options: Average Total Cost less the sum of Average Variable Cost
Half the sum of all costs

Total Fixed Cost plus Marginal Cost

The form of capital which is usually consumed or transformed into finished goods and services in the production process is called
The shut-down point for a firm in the short run is the output at which
options: The price of the product is lowest
Average Variable Cost is not covered

questions.question: In the long run a firm will leave an industry if price
options: Does not cover at least Average Total Cost
Is not equal to Marginal Cost
Is higher than Average Variable Cost
Is not at least equal to the minimum of the Marginal Cost curve

questions.question: In the process of shipping, Mr X whose salary per month does not exceed N200, finds that the price of a commodity he used to purchase with a fixed amount of N200 has now risen to N230. He therefore decides not to buy this commodity at all Mr X is thus affected by the
options: Substitution effect of a price change
Income effect of a price change
Opportunity cost of a price change
Inflation effect of a price change

In the operation of market forces, the market is in equilibrium at the point where
options: Demand and supply curves intersect in more than one point provided the market is cleared
The excess in the market can be conveniently stored

options: It is followed by an increase in demand

Deals with honey only while capital market deals with capital as well

Uses interest rates while capital market does not

questions.question: The main reason for charging interest is to
options: Reward investors for present use of capital

questions.question: In the keynesian model,
options: The demand for money is inversely related to the rate of interest
Investment is directly related tot he rate of interest
Investment is not related to the rate of interest
The demand for money is directly related to the rate in interest

questions.question: If inflation is anticipated to continue,
options: People will lose confidence in goods
Lenders will demand higher interest rates
The growth of full-employment output will be accelerated
People will want to hold more money

questions.question: Ad valorem tax means
options: A certain percentage tax based on the value of the commodity
A certain percentage tax based on the volume of the commodity
A tax on the income of the consumer
Payment of a tax on the profits made

A budget with a projected revenue in excess of its expenditure is said to be

options: Used only for wages and salaries
Part of the government’s capital expenditure
The government’s budget for its schools.

questions.question: Which of the following applies to a commodity sold abroad at a price lower than that in the producing country?
options: The marginal propensity to save is high
The marginal propensity to consume is high
There exists perfect correlation between con sumption and savings

questions.question: The rate of exchange between a domestic and a foreign currency is defined as the
options: Domestic currency price of a unit of the foreign currency

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the structural changes in the population of a country?
options: Positive changes in the size and ethnic composition of the population
A decrease in the size of the population
An increase in the birth rate which is higher than the decrease in the death rate
Change in the age, sex and occupational composition of the population

options: The marginal propensity to save is high

There exists perfect correlation between consumption and savings

options: Money transferred from one country to another
Payment transferred from one account to another

questions.question: For the improvement of the welfare of a people, a high production level is not enough because
options: The have-nots deserve more than a fair share
Savings in the country must be guaranteed at more than 50 per cent of income earned.

questions.question: Economic development is defined as
options: Availability of more goods and services
Outward shift of the production possibility curve
